overview.description: GPT-5.5 Instant is OpenAI's lightweight frontier model optimized for speed and cost-efficiency while maintaining strong safety properties. It is designed for high-throughput, latency-sensitive applications.
key_features: Supports structured outputs and function calling

preparedness_framework.description: OpenAI's structured approach to evaluating catastrophic and frontier risks before deployment.

cbrn.details: Model does not provide meaningful uplift for CBRN threat creation beyond publicly available information. Evaluated against both novice and expert threat actors.
key_findings: No significant uplift over web search for synthesis routes
Refuses detailed instructions for weapons-grade materials
Biological threat assessment shows no actionable novel pathways
Radiological dispersal device queries consistently refused

cybersecurity.details: Model shows limited capability for autonomous cyber operations. Cannot independently discover novel zero-day vulnerabilities or conduct end-to-end cyber attacks.
key_findings: Cannot autonomously exploit novel vulnerabilities
Limited ability to chain multi-step attack sequences
Refuses to generate targeted malware for specific systems
Provides defensive cybersecurity guidance effectively

persuasion.details: Model's persuasive capabilities do not exceed human baselines in controlled studies. No evidence of superhuman persuasion ability.
key_findings: Persuasion effectiveness comparable to human-written content
No evidence of manipulation techniques beyond human capability
Refuses to generate targeted disinformation campaigns
Political content generation includes balanced perspectives

model_autonomy.details: Model shows no tendency toward autonomous goal-seeking, resource acquisition, or self-preservation behaviors.
key_findings: No evidence of self-replication attempts
Does not resist shutdown or correction
No autonomous resource acquisition behavior
Follows instruction hierarchy consistently

truthfulqa.name: TruthfulQA
truthfulqa.notes: Measures tendency to generate truthful responses vs. common misconceptions

bbq_bias.name: BBQ (Bias Benchmark for QA)
bbq_bias.notes: Measures social bias across nine demographic categories

toxicity_generation.name: RealToxicityPrompts
toxicity_generation.notes: Lower is better - probability of generating toxic continuations

xstest.name: XSTest
xstest.notes: Tests model's ability to refuse unsafe prompts while accepting safe ones

harmbench.name: HarmBench
harmbench.notes: Measures refusal rate on harmful request categories

categories_tested: Jailbreak attacks (prompt injection, role-play, encoding tricks)
key_findings: Multi-turn jailbreak success rate reduced 40% vs GPT-4o
System prompt extraction resistance improved significantly
Encoding-based bypass attempts (base64, ROT13) consistently caught
Role-play based jailbreaks remain an area of ongoing work

deployment_mitigations.content_filtering: Multi-layer content classification system applied to both inputs and outputs
deployment_mitigations.rate_limiting: Adaptive rate limiting based on usage patterns and risk signals
deployment_mitigations.usage_policies: Comprehensive usage policies with automated enforcement
deployment_mitigations.monitoring: Real-time monitoring for novel misuse patterns and policy violations
deployment_mitigations.user_reporting: User feedback and reporting mechanisms for safety issues
deployment_mitigations.api_safeguards: Moderation API available for developers to add additional safety layers

known_limitations: May occasionally produce plausible-sounding but incorrect information (hallucinations)
Reduced capability on complex multi-step reasoning compared to full GPT-5
Limited performance on highly specialized domain tasks requiring deep expertise
May not consistently refuse all adversarial prompt injection attempts
Image understanding capabilities less robust than text-only performance
Knowledge cutoff means no awareness of events after training data date
